text: The Ohio Court of Appeals, Eleventh Appellate District, sanctioned an attorney after a non-attorney paralegal used ChatGPT to generate fabricated quotations that the attorney submitted in a criminal appeal. The attorney admitted the AI use and the court found the conduct sanctionable; it also noted the attorney had continued using ChatGPT after the sanctions hearing. Aggravating: a subsequent October 2025 filing visibly contained a ChatGPT prompt.
deployer: Respondent attorney (Ohio appellate counsel; name not stated in order)
canonical_source_conflicted: Non-existent judicial quotations attributed to real Ohio judges; Ohio Rules of Professional Conduct
mitigation_gap: Attorney failed to review paralegal's ChatGPT output before submission; continued AI use after sanctions hearing.
reliance_or_harm: $2,000 monetary fine (credit for $2,000 already paid to Ashtabula County Prosecutor); referral to Ohio Office of Disciplinary Counsel; removal as counsel; application to reopen stricken; two-year order to serve judgment on falsely-implicated judges.
